quickId: Reddish-brown; engorged adults are darker and rounded
Fecal spotting on seams and headboards
damageNotes: Bite reactions vary widely. Not a confirmed disease vector under typical exposure.
management: Integrated approach: encasements, heat, vacuuming, targeted residuals. Inspect-confirm before treatment.

quickId: Longer fringe hairs on pronotum (microscope needed)
Found near roosts, not beds, in initial infestations
Bites people only after bats leave
damageNotes: Indicator of a current or former bat colony in the structure.
management: Exclude bats first (legally and seasonally), then treat harborage. Treating without exclusion fails.
